dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for a reported seizure.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
My son had a grand mal seizure about one minute long. He had one just days ago at the hospital.
Thomas, can you tell me your name?
I'm... Thomas.
Thomas, can you tell me the name of the city we're in?
I think it's... [CITY]?
Thomas, there's some blood around your mouth. We need to keep that area clear and unstressed.
His breathing is 22 per minute and regular. No sign of labored breathing.
His pulse is 159 beats per minute.
Feeling around, the skin is clammy and the blood pressure is 151 over 69.
Oxygen saturation remains at 97% on room air.
Blood glucose shows up as normal at 136 mg/dL.
EKG results show sinus tachycardia.
His heart rate remains at 159.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Thomas, when was the last time you had a seizure like this before?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: He was released from the hospital about five days ago. They said the cause was related to this condition.
We'll check this blood around your mouth next. Besides that, any other injuries or pain?
Thomas, we'll be starting an IV in your arm-can we go through with that?
IV has been successfully placed into the left antecubital, no complications noted.
We're also setting up continuous EKG monitoring.
Four-lead EKG monitoring is now active and working.
Based on the findings, we're following the medical-seizure protocol 3.
After the seizure, his blood glucose has dropped to zero.
His heart rate is now slightly down to 144 beats per minute.
His breathing rate is 24 per minute now.
His blood pressure is 112 over 46. A bit lower than before.
His oxygen saturation is still at 96%.
On the GCS, Thomas has 14-eye (4), verbal (4), motor (6).
Thomas, are you experiencing any pain?
No, I don't feel any pain.
You're currently taking Divalproex Sodium and Paliperidone (Invega).
Thomas has a history of schizoaffective disorder and schizoid personality.
All protocol notes indicate the patient is stable for transport.
During transport, Thomas had a short 15-second tonic-clonic seizure. But the IV is still working.
Thomas, we'll be moving you carefully-can you be okay with all straps for your safety?
You're now safely on the stretcher, locked and ready for transport.
Another short seizure occurred en route. He has fresh oral trauma.
[COMPANY_NAME] will be the destination, as requested by his mother.
The patient, 27, has a recorded history of seizures and schizoaffective disorder.
He experienced two seizures-most recently during transport.
Arrival findings were alert but disoriented, with visible oral trauma.
Elevated heart rate and tachycardia were observed. The IV site was functional.
Oxygen levels were stable, but glucose levels dropped to zero post-seizure.
EKG monitoring has been running and stays active.
Thomas, can you identify the year we are in?
I... Think it's 2019?
No signs of airway blockage. Just keep talking, Thomas.
Respirations remain consistent. Not using extra effort to breathe.
EKG leads are still consistent with sinus tachycardia.
We note no known drug allergies recorded.
Thomas, do you take any medications on a regular basis?
Skin is otherwise unremarkable, aside from the characteristic oral injuries.
Head shows no signs of trauma. Pupils are equal and reactive.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Any hearing or smell issues after the seizure?

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Patient
dialogue.utterance: Not that I know of.
Heart sounds are stable. Chest rises evenly with breathing.
Abdomen is soft and non-tender. No distress here.
Any numbness or tingling in your fingers or toes, Thomas?
No, feels normal, I think.
No abnormal urinary behavior reported. Normal voiding.
EKG remains clear and on a sinus pattern.
Oxygen levels are consistent. No need for supplemental oxygen at this stage.
No new medications are being given during transit.
Thomas, on a scale of 1 to 10, would you say you're in any pain?
Zero. No pain.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Can you tell us about any other symptoms you might have experienced?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Just some confusion and sore mouth.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - last oral intake
dialogue.utterance: Thomas, was there anything you ate or drank before your seizure?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - last oral intake
dialogue.utterance: He had some soup about four hours ago.
Respiratory rate is stable at 22 breaths per minute.
Blood pressure is returning closer to baseline at 140 over 80.
Glucose levels have risen back into the normal range at 100 mg/dL.
Pulse rate is also trending down to 135 beats per minute.
All monitoring tools are active and our transport is going according to plan.
Thomas, I just want to check in-how are you doing?
I just feel tired but everything seems okay.
That's normal after a seizure. We're here to make sure you're safe.
[COMPANY_NAME] is our top choice for transport and we have a direct address loaded.
We continue to monitor IV and EKG throughout. No changes noted.
Thomas was taken from his home safely and remains stable during transit.
No acute trauma or new symptoms reported as of now.
IV, EKG, and vitals are within expected parameters.
Documented postictal state following the seizure, with no pain reported.
Seizures occurred twice, first upon arrival and again en route to the hospital.
Medical team at [COMPANY_NAME] has been alerted and is ready for arrival.
Thomas, it looks like we'll be arriving shortly. Are there any questions?
No, just a little nervous.
That's normal. We are here to make sure everything is taken care of.
We're monitoring you closely, so you'll know the moment anything changes.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Was there any change you noted in your behavior or mood before the seizure?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I was feeling tired but not anxious or anything.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Reassuring. We don't see signs of distress or anxiety.
We don't see signs of nuchal rigidity or neck stiffness.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Partner
dialogue.utterance: No nasal or ear drainage observed.
Chest wall is non-tender. Breath sounds are equal bilaterally.
Bowel sounds are present and non-tender.
Did you experience any bladder or bowel control issues during the seizure?
No. I feel fine in this area.
Thomas remains stable and alert. We are minutes from the hospital.
Still at 22 breaths per minute, no dyspnea observed.
Blood pressure is still within safe range at 138 over 82.
Heart rate is currently stable at 130.
Oxygen saturation is strong at 97%.
Everything so far is consistent with the transport protocol.
We continue IV site monitoring and EKG leads are intact throughout transport.
Thomas will need further evaluation upon arrival.
Neuro and psychiatric evaluation will likely be needed after initial assessment.
We are visually spotting and arriving at the hospital via rear emergency entrance.
Thomas, you're almost home. Keep resting and we'll get you the rest of the way.
All initial assessments and interventions are complete and detailed in our report.
Our destinations were informed of condition and staging area is prepared.
Ambulance is now staged at receiving ED bay.
Unloading Thomas now, with full assistance of hospital crew.
Ensuring no movement during transfer and all straps fully released.
Thomas is handed off and receiving care from [COMPANY_NAME] staff.
Team at [COMPANY_NAME] has been briefed and will handle next steps.
All equipment, documents, and notes are ready for handover.
Thomas arrives for further care.
Units return to service after clean-up and administrative tasks.
